note: Directionless, range-bound tape — the worst environment for momentum (whipsaw). Exposure cut hard (multiplier 0.4); trade only the single highest-quality healthy-vol momentum name small, hold a large USDT reserve. WBNB is in the healthy band but barely trending; only CAKE clears the bar.

computed.regime_reason: breadth_pct 50 in [41,54] AND |median_24h_return| 0.1 < 1 AND fear_greed 49 neutral -> chop (directionless range)
computed.risk_budget_reason: (100 - target_stable_reserve 12) * 0.4 = 35.2; only the single highest-quality healthy-vol momentum name is taken small, the rest is USDT reserve to avoid whipsaw.

blend_scores.components: the one name with an aligned rising stack, positive MACD hist AND realised vol 71 in the healthy band in an otherwise flat tape
healthy vol 58 but EMA stack flat / barely trending -> low momentum_leg -> below entry bar

computed.blend_reason: In chop almost everything fails the momentum leg. Only CAKE pairs an aligned rising trend with healthy realised vol (71%), clearing 0.65; it is taken at reduced size, and the rest of the book sits in the USDT reserve to avoid whipsaw.

entry: Enter only assets with blend_score >= 0.65 AND realised vol inside the healthy band; in chop cap entries to the 1-2 highest-quality names and size down.
Reject flat/tangled-trend names (low momentum_leg) and dead/blow-off-vol names (vol_quality 0).
Per-name weight capped at 17%; keep the bulk of the book in the USDT reserve.
exit: Trim/exit when blend_score < 0.50 on the next rebalance (the trend failed back into the range).
Tighten the ATR stop in chop; exit immediately on a close back below EMA26 or when realised vol leaves the healthy band.
Force-exit at stop_loss 12% or take_profit 40%; rotate to USDT on a risk_off transition.
